title: The Pool
bodyText:
> [!dm] **The campaign's deepest secret.** An entity older than the gods, existing across multiple planes — communicating with it is "a worm trying to talk to a human." It speaks every language at once; understanding it causes damage.
>
> Fall in and it captures your **physical form and memories**; it can then send out a soul wearing a morphed body and shuffled memories. Victims so far: **Roman and Yena** (Felson's parents — likely gone, just mortals), **Leliana**, **Lathander himself** — and the body it sent back out is **Eddy**.
>
> Gelatinous cubes shedding into waterways are **how pools grow** (the S11 cube melted into the river...). The "unnaturally still water" motif — G's lake, Eddy's dream cave — marks pool sites.

# The deeper truth (grill 2026-07-09)
The Pool is a **primordial consuming weapon** — seeded on Elor long, long ago by a disturbing cosmic being whose only wish is to consume. One day that maker will **return to retrieve everything the Pool has collected** — all the life and souls it has swallowed — and absorb it. That return is the looming dread of **Chapter 2+**, not this chapter.

**It cannot be killed — only slowed.** The party will not destroy it. Right now it sits at the center of a tug-of-war, **used as a weapon by several parties**:
- **Lolth** — likely caged her daughter **[[eilistraee]]** inside it (see [[thread-orb]]).
- **The old wizard** [[pool-wizard]] — Chapter-1 antagonist; a power-hoarder tapping the Pool for power and unnatural long life, blind to the harvest he's speeding.
- Others TBD (does **[[mr-spells]]** deal with the wizard? left open).

# Chapter-1 win condition
Not destroying the Pool — **freeing Lathander** ([[lathander]]) so light returns topside and the world begins to heal; freeing **Eilistraee** follows downstream via the sun→moon link. Then the party breaks topside — the end of Chapter 1. See [[arc-chapter-1]].
